data.title: DoubleU offers $184M to acquire remaining DoubleDown Interactive stake
data.description:
• DoubleU Games to acquire all remaining publicly held ADSs of DoubleDown Interactive (DDI) in an all‑cash transaction.
  • DoubleU Games is offering $11.25 in cash per ADS, valuing the transaction at around $184M. Each ADS represents 1/20 of one DDI common share, so the offer effectively covers the ~32.95% of DDI’s common shares not already owned by DoubleU.
  • If completed, DDI would become a wholly owned subsidiary of DoubleU Games, de‑listed from the Nasdaq.
  • DoubleU Games currently holds ~67.1% of DDI's outstanding common shares.
  • The stock price jumps about 12% on Tuesday during pre-market hours.

data.title: China’s Q1 urban job creation hits 2.99M as unemployment steadies at 5.3%
data.description:
China added 2.99 million new urban jobs in the first quarter of 2026, keeping the surveyed urban unemployment rate at an average of 5.3%, according to the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Security.

The performance remains well within Beijing's full-year target of approximately 5.5% unemployment and over 12 million new jobs.

Ministry spokesperson Cui Pengcheng noted that efforts are currently focused on the "critical window" for college graduates entering the market. To support this, the government facilitated 59,000 job fairs offering 36 million positions and provided 1 million transportation trips to ensure migrant worker mobility during the spring recruitment season.

On Tuesday, the Shanghai Composite edged down 0.1% to 4,082, while the Shenzhen Component fell 0.8% to 14,869.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

data.title: European markets flat on stalled U.S.-Iran talks
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.17% to 10,339. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.05% to 24,073. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.01% to 8,140. 

In other parts of Europe, retail trade in Spain rose by 4.1% Y/Y in March. Spain’s unemployment rate climbed to 10.83% in Q1.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.19% lower to 607.8, marking a seventh straight session of losses. The investor sentiment remained cautious as tensions in the Middle East persisted, the Strait of Hormuz stayed largely closed, and oil prices continued to climb. The euro held near $1.17, lingering close to the two-week lows seen last week, as investors assessed the fallout from a diplomatic standoff in U.S.-Iran negotiations.

On the corporate front, Novartis (NVS) shares fell after reporting sales below expectations. BP (BP) rose as its profit more than doubled, while Barclays (BCS) dropped.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 2 basis points to 4.36%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 5.00%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 3.05%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: U.S. is being humiliated by Iran, lacks strategy in negotiations - Germany's Merz
data.description:
German Chancellor ‌Friedrich Merz has said the Iranian leadership is humiliating the U.S., which "quite obviously went into this war without any strategy," amid a lack of progress in negotiations to end the conflict.

"The Iranians are obviously negotiating very skilfully — or simply very skilfully not negotiating," Merz said during a visit to a school in western Germany. "A whole nation is being humiliated by the Iranian leadership."

He added that the Americans "have no truly convincing strategy in the negotiations," pointing to U.S. officials traveling to Pakistan earlier this month and leaving without results.

Merz reiterated that Europe was not consulted before the U.S. and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran on Feb. 28, saying he expressed his skepticism directly to Trump afterward.

"If I'd known that this would go on for five or six weeks and get progressively worse, I would've made my point to him more forcefully," the chancellor ‌said. "The problem with conflicts like this is always the same – you don't just get in, you also have to get out. We saw that very painfully in Afghanistan and Iraq."

Merz added that the war has directly impacted Germany's economy and is "costing us a great deal of money," calling for an end to the war "as soon as possible."

His unusually sharp rebuke comes as U.S.-Iran negotiations make little headway. Iran offered to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if the U.S. lifted its blockade of the country's ports and postponed nuclear talks. But the U.S. is reportedly unwilling to accept the offer.

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report.

data.title: ING sees Brent hitting $150 on re-escalation of Hormuz crisis
data.description:
ING raised its oil price outlook, citing a prolonged disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and a slower-than-expected recovery in flows, as geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran show no signs of easing.

“In our base case, we initially assumed that we would start to see a gradual resumption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz in April. However, this has clearly not materialised. Therefore, we are updating our base case assumptions and, as a result, revising higher our ICE Brent forecasts,” ING said.

 

Base case: ING now assumes a gradual resumption of flows starting in May and June, with volumes staying below pre-war levels for most of the year. Under this scenario, ICE Brent is expected to average $104/bbl ($96 previously) over Q2, while the significant inventory drawdown and slow recovery towards pre-war flows sees Brent averaging $92/bbl ($88/bbl previously) over Q4.

Prolonged disruption: A longer-lasting constraint on flows would keep the market tighter for longer, with Brent likely holding a floor above $100/bbl for the remainder of the year as supply shortages persist.

Re-escalation: ING highlights a more severe risk where renewed escalation further disrupts regional exports, including Saudi shipments via the Red Sea and UAE exports from Fujairah. Renewed escalation could nearly halt oil supply through the end of the second quarter, ING said, pushing oil prices to new record highs.

Brent Futures (CO1:COM) were up +3.08% at press time to $111.57/barrel, while Crude Oil Futures (CL1:COM) were trading at $98.99/bbl, up +2.72%.

Crude oil futures closed higher Monday, with Brent crude rising for the sixth straight session, as the oil market doubts the Trump administration will accept a new Iranian proposal to open the Strait of Hormuz on the condition that the U.S. lift its blockade and postpone negotiations related to Iran's nuclear activities.

Goldman Sachs also raised its year-end oil price forecasts, now expecting Brent crude to average $90/bbl and WTI to average $83/bbl, up from previous estimates of $80/bbl and $75/bbl, respectively.

data.title: Samsung SDI narrows loss; shares jump on ESS demand
data.description:
South Korean battery maker Samsung SDI reported a smaller-than-expected Q1 loss, helping its shares extend a sharp rally. The stock jumped as much as 7% in early Seoul trading, driven by optimism around the company’s strategic pivot toward ESS for AI data centers and supply agreements with global automakers.

Samsung SDI reported Q1 2026 consolidated revenue of about 3.58T won (roughly $2.6B), up 12.6% Y/Y. Its operating loss narrowed sharply to 155.6B won, about 64% smaller than the same‑quarter loss a year earlier, coming in better than expectations.

The battery business saw resilient demand for grid‑scale energy storage systems (ESS)). Earnings were further aided by 80.5B won in U.S. advanced manufacturing tax credits tied to ESS production.

data.title: Komatsu reports FY results; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Komatsu press release (KMTUY): FY net income attributable decreased by 14.4% from the previous fiscal year to JPY 376.4 billion.
  • Revenue of JPY 4132.75B (+0.7% Y/Y).
  • The operating income ratio decreased by 2.3 percentage points from the previous fiscal year to 13.7%.

data.title: Greenland Energy prices $70M offering at $4 per share
data.description:
• Greenland Energy (GLND) priced a public offering of 17.5M shares at a price of $4.00 per share.
  • Each common share is being sold in combination with an accompanying common stock warrant. Each Warrant is immediately exercisable, will entitle the holder to purchase one common share at an exercise price of $5.00 per share, and will expire five years from the date of issuance.
  • Gross proceeds are expected to be $70M. The warrants have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Market and are expected to commence trading under the symbol "GLNDW" on April 28, 2026.
  • The offering is expected to close on April 29, 2026.
